skill.name: artifacts-builder
skill.description: Builds multi-component Claude HTML artifacts by scaffolding React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui projects, then bundling them into a single shareable HTML file.
skill.summary: Create complex Claude HTML artifacts with a React and shadcn/ui scaffold.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found no prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or confirmed malicious intent. The weak cryptography and documentation URL detections are false positives from license text, CSS theme values, and Markdown examples. Confirmed risk is operational: shell scripts install packages, create project files, extract a bundled component archive, and remove fixed build outputs.

medium_findings.title: Project Bootstrap Executes Package Managers
medium_findings.description: The init and bundle scripts run pnpm and npm commands to create projects and install dependencies. This is expected for a frontend artifact builder, but it creates network and supply-chain exposure when executed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and are central to the skill behavior. They appear legitimate, but they fetch and install external packages.

medium_findings.title: Generated Project Files Are Written and Modified
medium_findings.description: The init script writes configuration files, modifies TypeScript configuration, updates Vite configuration, and extracts local UI components into the generated project. This is expected scaffolding behavior, but users should run it only in intended project directories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct file writes and edits are visible in the script. The behavior is consistent with project generation, not hidden persistence.

medium_findings.title: Build Cleanup Removes Fixed Output Paths
medium_findings.description: The bundling script removes the dist directory and bundle.html before rebuilding. The paths are fixed and not attacker-controlled, but accidental data loss is possible if a user stores unrelated files at those paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The rm command is explicit and uses fixed names. Risk is limited because there is no variable expansion in the removal target.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high static detections map to Apache License wording, CSS color names and HSL values, and skill description text. No hashing, encryption, random generation, or credential handling code was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these lines are prose or CSS theme values. There is no cryptographic API or security-sensitive algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Alerts Are Documentation or Schema References
low_findings.description: The URL findings point to the Apache License, shadcn/ui documentation, and a components.json schema reference. No outbound request with user data or credentials was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are static references in license text, generated configuration, or documentation. No code sends data to them.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Are Expected Skill Instructions
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md command alerts are shell examples that tell the assistant how to run the included scripts. They are not hidden execution paths, but users should understand that following the skill will run local shell scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible documentation and match the skill purpose. The risk is transparent execution, not covert command injection.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Text Matches
low_findings.description: The static reconnaissance alerts are caused by ordinary wording about design guidance, theming systems, and optional testing tools. No host enumeration, secret discovery, or environment harvesting behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these lines are prose. They do not instruct or implement system scanning.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Manager Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts execute npm, pnpm, pnpm create, pnpm install, and pnpm exec. This is needed for the skill, but it should be treated as code execution with network dependency risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The package manager calls are explicit. The risk depends on registry content and the execution environment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Removal of Build Outputs
dangerous_patterns.description: The bundling script uses rm -rf on dist and bundle.html before rebuilding. The targets are fixed, which lowers command-injection risk, but the command can remove existing files with those names.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The deletion command is present and its targets are clear. No attacker-controlled path is evident.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Archive Extraction Into Source Tree
dangerous_patterns.description: The init script extracts a bundled components archive into the generated src directory. This is expected for shipping shadcn/ui components, but archive extraction is a sensitive filesystem operation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tar extraction is explicit and uses a bundled archive. No absolute or parent-directory archive paths were observed during review.

content.value_statement: Complex Claude artifacts often need routing, state, and a full component system. This skill scaffolds a React and shadcn/ui project, then bundles it into one shareable HTML artifact.
actual_capabilities: Creates a React 18 and TypeScript project with Vite.
Configures Tailwind CSS 3.4.1 with shadcn/ui theme variables.
Installs Radix UI and related shadcn/ui dependencies.
Extracts prepackaged shadcn/ui components into the generated project.
Adds TypeScript and Vite path aliases for the src directory.
Bundles the finished project into one self-contained HTML file.
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or newer and a working package manager environment.
Downloads packages from external registries during setup and bundling.
Best suited for complex artifacts, not simple static HTML snippets.
Does not provide automated quality checks beyond the generated build workflow.

use_cases.title: Prototype Interactive Dashboards
use_cases.description: Create a React artifact with tables, tabs, dialogs, and controls for reviewing product concepts inside Claude.

use_cases.title: Build Shareable Engineering Demos
use_cases.description: Scaffold a TypeScript app, implement a focused demo, and bundle it as one HTML artifact for review.

use_cases.title: Create Internal Workflow Tools
use_cases.description: Use prebuilt UI components to assemble calculators, planners, and process tools that can be shared as artifacts.

prompt_templates.title: Start a New Artifact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use artifacts-builder to create a new React artifact project named planning-tool. Keep the first screen focused on the core workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Add shadcn Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Use artifacts-builder to build an artifact that uses tabs, cards, dialogs, and form controls for a customer intake workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Bundle an Existing Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use artifacts-builder to bundle the current React artifact project into one HTML file and report the output file name.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Complex App Artifact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use artifacts-builder to scaffold a multi-view React artifact with state management, shadcn/ui components, and a final single-file HTML bundle.

output_examples.input: Create a project for a sprint planning artifact.
output_examples.output: A new React and TypeScript project is created with Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui components, and path aliases ready for implementation.

output_examples.input: Bundle the completed artifact for sharing.
output_examples.output: The project is built, assets are inlined, and a single HTML file is produced for use as a Claude artifact.

output_examples.input: Build a dashboard-style artifact with reusable controls.
output_examples.output: The generated app can use installed UI components such as tabs, tables, dialogs, inputs, and buttons.

best_practices: Run the scripts in a dedicated working directory created for the artifact.
Use this skill when the artifact needs multiple components, state, or a modern React toolchain.
Review package installation output before sharing or deploying the bundled artifact.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for a simple one-file HTML artifact.
Do not run the bundling script in a directory that stores unrelated dist or bundle.html files.
Do not assume external package installs are safe in restricted or offline environments.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui project for complex Claude HTML artifacts.

faq.question: Does it work with Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Why does it need shell scripts?
faq.answer: The scripts create the frontend project, install dependencies, configure files, and bundle the final artifact.

faq.question: Does it access the network?
faq.answer: Yes. It installs packages from external registries during setup and bundling.

faq.question: Is it suitable for simple HTML output?
faq.answer: No. It is intended for larger artifacts that benefit from React, state, and reusable UI components.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: Users should treat the scripts as local code execution that installs dependencies and writes files.
